page_title: Income requirements for the sponsor - Canada.ca

h2: Income required for the 3 tax years before you apply (for the 2025 intake)
h3: Examples of how to calculate your family size

sections.heading: Income required for the 3 tax years before you apply (for the 2025 intake)
If you live in Quebec
items: You must meet Quebec's income requirements .

sections.heading: Family size
items: Family size is counted on a yearly basis. Count each person in the year they became part of your family. If someone is no longer part of your family when you apply (for example, because of death, legal divorce, or an expired undertaking), don’t include them in your family size for any of the 3 tax years.
To count the number of people you’ll be responsible for, include the following:
You must include all the principal applicant’s family members, even if they
bullets: The people in your family at the time of application . This includes yourself (the sponsor) your spouse (even if you’re separated, in most cases) or common-law partner If your spouse or common-law partner is co-signing your application, include them for all 3 years. If they aren’t co-signing, only include them for the years they meet the definition of a spouse or common-law partner . your dependent children, the dependent children of your spouse or common-law partner and any dependent children of the dependent children (if they have any) any other person you and your co-signer (if you have one) sponsored or co-signed, for whom an undertaking is still in effect Include the family members (for example, spouse, common-law partner, or dependent children) of the person you sponsored or co-signed for, whether or not they were included in the undertaking.
yourself (the sponsor)

your spouse (even if you’re separated, in most cases) or common-law partner If your spouse or common-law partner is co-signing your application, include them for all 3 years. If they aren’t co-signing, only include them for the years they meet the definition of a spouse or common-law partner .
If your spouse or common-law partner is co-signing your application, include them for all 3 years.
If they aren’t co-signing, only include them for the years they meet the definition of a spouse or common-law partner .
your dependent children, the dependent children of your spouse or common-law partner and any dependent children of the dependent children (if they have any)

any other person you and your co-signer (if you have one) sponsored or co-signed, for whom an undertaking is still in effect Include the family members (for example, spouse, common-law partner, or dependent children) of the person you sponsored or co-signed for, whether or not they were included in the undertaking.
Include the family members (for example, spouse, common-law partner, or dependent children) of the person you sponsored or co-signed for, whether or not they were included in the undertaking.

The people you want to sponsor and their family at the time of application . This includes the person you’re sponsoring (principal applicant) the principal applicant’s spouse or common-law partner Include their separated spouse unless either the principal applicant or their spouse is in a common-law relationship with another person. the principal applicant’s dependent children, the dependent children of their spouse or common-law partner and any dependent children of the dependent children (if they have any)
the person you’re sponsoring (principal applicant)
the principal applicant’s spouse or common-law partner Include their separated spouse unless either the principal applicant or their spouse is in a common-law relationship with another person.
Include their separated spouse unless either the principal applicant or their spouse is in a common-law relationship with another person.
the principal applicant’s dependent children, the dependent children of their spouse or common-law partner and any dependent children of the dependent children (if they have any)
aren’t coming to Canada
are permanent residents of Canada or Canadian citizens

sections.heading: Examples of how to calculate your family size
Proof of income
items: To prove you meet the income requirements, you must provide a notice of assessment from the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) for each of the 3 tax years before the date you apply.
There are 2 ways you can do this:
bullets: Give us permission to get your tax information directly from CRA On the form Financial Evaluation for Parents and Grandparents Sponsorship [IMM 5768] (PDF, 171 KB) , under question 8, “Sponsor Statement of Consent”: Check “Yes.” Provide your social insurance number (SIN), signature and date.

On the form Financial Evaluation for Parents and Grandparents Sponsorship [IMM 5768] (PDF, 171 KB) , under question 8, “Sponsor Statement of Consent”: Check “Yes.” Provide your social insurance number (SIN), signature and date.
Check “Yes.”
Provide your social insurance number (SIN), signature and date.

Send us the paper copies of your notices of assessment Complete the form Income Sources for the Sponsorship of Parents and Grandparents [IMM 5748] (PDF, 154 KB) . Include the notices of assessment with your application package. If you don’t have the paper copies, log in to CRA’s My Account to find them online.
Complete the form Income Sources for the Sponsorship of Parents and Grandparents [IMM 5748] (PDF, 154 KB) .
Include the notices of assessment with your application package. If you don’t have the paper copies, log in to CRA’s My Account to find them online.
